routing_rule._doc: v2 router. Two changes vs v1 (hybrid-grep-4k-rtk-err-cat-v1): (1) budget raised from 4k to 120k tokens, sized against the abstain cliff observed empirically — at the 13-case state, Sonnet/Haiku scored grep cleanly at ≤100k tokens (airflow 0.717 at 100864, biome 0.550 at 98656) but abstained at ≥161k (rust 0.000 at 161086, nodejs 0.000 at 359459). 120k is conservative buffer. (2) Fallback switched from rtk-err-cat to tail-200, motivated by the §3c finding that rtk-err-cat ALSO collapses on density-driven contexts (320k tokens on nodejs) where tail's bounded 200-line window survives. The trade-off: tail loses on middle-signal cases, but those are exactly the cases where grep fits the 120k budget. The 13-case Phase 3 analysis (§3d) showed that no individual baseline wins across both signal positions; this router picks the right one via the budget proxy.

anti_leakage._doc: Same anti-leakage as v1: router reads only the grep + tail manifest rows for the case (case_id, context_path, output_byte_size). The 120k budget was tuned against v2 but does NOT use ground_truth or eval_* outputs — it was set from the empirical Sonnet/Haiku abstain-vs-non-abstain boundary in eval_diagnosis_*.json output, which itself never reads ground_truth. The threshold is calibrated, not selected — it would generalize to any reasoning-budget-constrained debugger.
